title: Graph points
description: Plot a given point on the coordinate plane.
topic_path: Module 6: Problem solving with the coordinate plane

hints:
The **ordered pair** of a point tells us how to get to it from the origin.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{array}{cc}
(\blueD{\text{distance right}},& \greenD{\text{distance up}})\\
\swarrow&\searrow\\
\blueD{x\text{-coordinate}}&\greenD{y\text{-coordinate}}
\end{array}$
So, for $( \blueD{6}, \greenD{4})$, our $\blueD{x\text{-coordinate}}$ is $\blueD{6}$, and our $\greenD{y\text{-coordinate}}$ is $\greenD{4}$.
Let's start by going to $\blueD{6}$ on the $x$-axis:



[[☃ image 1]]

Now we go up to $\greenD{4}$ on the $y$-axis, and plot the point $(\blueD{6}, \greenD{4})$:


[[☃ image 1]]

Here is $(6,4)$ plotted on the coordinate plane.



[[☃ image 1]]

The **ordered pair** of a point tells us how to get to it from the origin.

[[☃ image 1]]
